creator: adamk@voicenet.com
text:
I updated my ports tree recently and did a portupgrade on pretty much 
everything.  Now neverball, bzflag, supertux are all crashing again, and 
it seems to be the same issue:

(gdb) run
Starting program: /usr/local/bin/supertux
(no debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...(no 
debugging symbols found)...(no debugging symbols found)...[New LWP 100472]
[New Thread 28804140 (LWP 100472)]
Couldn't read /proc/self/exe, using default path: /usr/local/share/supertux
Datadir: /usr/local/share/supertux
[New Thread 288043c0 (LWP 100598)]
[Thread 288043c0 (LWP 100598) exited]
[New Thread 28804500 (LWP 100598)]
Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
[Switching to Thread 28804140 (LWP 100472)]
0x284f0ca2 in malloc_usable_size () from /lib/libc.so.7
(gdb) bt
#0  0x284f0ca2 in malloc_usable_size () from /lib/libc.so.7
#1  0x284f1cda in free () from /lib/libc.so.7
#2  0x281c1b8a in SDL_SYS_JoystickClose () from 
/usr/local/lib/libSDL-1.2.so.11
#3  0x28908340 in ?? ()
#4  0x00000000 in ?? ()
#5  0x281cc1e0 in __JCR_LIST__ () from /usr/local/lib/libSDL-1.2.so.11
#6  0x00000000 in ?? ()
#7  0x00000000 in ?? ()
#8  0xbfbfe378 in ?? ()
#9  0x281c2666 in SDL_SYS_JoystickInit () from 
/usr/local/lib/libSDL-1.2.so.11
Previous frame inner to this frame (corrupt stack?)

Time to reopen this PR, I guess.

I am now using 9.0-CURRENT i386, updated and compiled on 02/02/2010.

Adam
